title: Chapter 20
text: Chapter Seventeen L ara slowly came to awareness after waking. Her eyelids were too heavy to open, eyes tired and irritated, her limbs leaden, and there was a strange ache between her legs. Had she had another of those dreams? They’d been vivid over the last few days, especially during Ronin’s absence, but she refused to touch herself. She was too conflicted about her desires . Inhaling deeply, she stretched, stilling abruptly when she realized there was someone beside her, body tucked against hers. She opened her eyes and lifted her head. Ronin lay on his back, one arm resting atop his chest and the other beneath her neck. He didn’t move, didn’t speak . Did bots sleep? She’d never seen Ronin in his bed since she came here, and the only time the bedding had even been disturbed was when she sat on it to sew his shirt . She glanced around the room. Enough gray, early morning light streamed in through the window for her to make out the dark shapes of the furniture. They’d slept together. That was the simplest way to look at it, even though there wasn’t anything simple about it . Lara sat up, tugging the blanket over her bare breasts. She was in Ronin’s room, in his bed , with him. Last night hadn’t been a dream . Tabitha was dead . Lara had spent much of the night crying. Even now, more tears threatened, and she did all she could to prevent them. They couldn’t bring Tabitha back . Crying only made her feel like a weak, sniveling, pathetic human being . But damn it, it hurt like a bitch . Suck it up . A teardrop trekked down her cheek. Lara wiped at it angrily, returning her gaze to Ronin . I buried her. West of town. Away from…all this. Now, Tabitha is free . She didn’t know many humans who would have done the same. A dead body meant potential items to claim, or — for the most desperate — a meal. Burial required time and energy that most were unwilling to expend for someone already gone . She reached out to place her hand on his chest, but hesitated with her palm hovering an inch away from his skin. Finally, she gave in and lowered her hand, appreciating his warmth and solidness . “Thank you,” she said softly. He was a bot; what could he have known about tending the dead? But he had, because Lara cared deeply for Tabitha . “For what?” he asked . Lara jumped, pulling her hand back. “I didn’t mean to wake you .” He lifted his head and opened his eyes, brow creasing. “I’ve been awake for one hundred and eighty-five years …” “So…you weren’t sleeping just now ?” “Bot’s don’t sleep, Lara Brooks. We go into low-power operations when necessary, but usually optics and audio still function in that state, albeit at a lower quality. There’s…” He dropped his head back, pupils dilating. “There’s on or off. Nothing in between, for us .” “Oh. Is that…is that what you were doing? The low-power thing ?” “ No .” “You just laid here all night with me ?” “ Yes .” Lara stared at him. He’d once told her that he went into the Dust seeking his purpose, because otherwise he would stare at the walls until he shut himself off. Then he’d be just like the furniture here, collecting dust, slowly breaking down as the building crumbled around him. How could watching her all night be any more exciting than watching paint peel off a wall ? “ Why ?” “Because I enjoyed being near you. Enjoyed holding you. Hearing your slow breaths and the rhythm of your heart. Every other night I can remember, I’ve spent alone .” Heat blossomed on her cheeks, spreading down to her neck and chest. She looked away, knowing he had fixed her with that penetrating gaze. He laid with her all night. Awake. A week ago, she would’ve thought it strange and unsettling. Now, it was comforting, especially after the hell of being alone in this house for two days . Every sound had made her skin crawl with fear, had added to the ball of dread in her gut. What little sleep she managed had been restless. Though she’d wanted to deny it, she felt safe when he was around, and knowing that he’d been with her all night long … “I was saying thanks for what you did for Tabitha. I know I didn’t, last night, so I wanted you to know it means a lot to me .” “Never buried anyone before,” he said after a long pause. “It seemed…right. The bot will remain for centuries, perhaps longer, but your sister…she’ll become part of something larger .” “You get it.” She smiled softly, sadly, and shook her head. “I don’t think any of the others do. Don’t think they can .” Ronin sat up, bed groaning beneath him, and turned to face her. The green of his eyes was darker in the diffused light. “I understand that organics break down over time and are reclaimed by the earth. I understand that all of this is important to you. And…I think I am beginning to understand why .” Lara’s gaze dropped, roaming over his bare torso. She noticed again the discolored patches of skin. “What are those ?” He looked down. The differences in shades were subtle, but she could make it out even in this dim lighting . “The closest I can get to scars,” he replied . She reached forward and touched one of the spots. Her fingers glided over his skin, feeling no difference between the mismatched portions . “Been through a lot of places,” he said. “Not all of them have the means to reskin synths. Some of the ones that do don’t have the resources to match pigmentation. I don’t notice it much, anymore. It’ll all be replaced again, eventually .” “Are they all from bullets?” She touched another one. It was likely her imagination, but it felt like his skin warmed with the contact . “Bullets, knives, metal rods, rocks. This one,” he placed his hand over hers, guiding it to the spot where a human’s heart was located, “was from a steel beam in a dust storm. It punched through two millimeters to the side of my power cell .” “And you survived through all of that.” She was awed, even as she cringed inwardly at the damage he’d suffered. At the pain he must’ve endured . His other hand slid beneath the blanket. Lara’s heart fluttered as he ran his palm over her thigh, around the back of her knee, and to her calf. It settled over a long, crescent-shaped scar there . “We’ve all survived our own trials,” he said. His fingertip trailed over the slightly raised skin . Her fingers curled against his chest, and she pressed her lips together, focusing on his words, but her body responded to his touch and the heat trailed in its wake . “I was salvaging,” she said, “and I don’t remember if I slipped or tripped, but I landed on a broken beam. Didn’t feel it at first, but there was a sliver as big as a knife stuck in my leg. Then it hurt like a bitch. Tabitha got all the bits of wood out and we cleaned it up as best we could, but I still got sick .” She lifted her knee as he continued to stroke the scar. “Like real sick. I can’t remember much of it, but Tabitha said I almost died. If it weren’t for her, I would have .” “Bots were created to endure,” Ronin said. “Sometimes it seems like humans were created to suffer. I’m more impressed by your survival than by mine .” “You trying to flatter me?” She smirked, and his hand paused . “Flattery isn’t a function I’m familiar with. It implies a level of dishonesty. I’m simply speaking the truth as I perceive it .” “Flattery can be true, if you mean it. Doesn’t hurt to tell a girl something you admire about her. You should try it sometime. Maybe you — ” “ Everything .” “What?” Lara’s throat was suddenly dry . “Everything I’ve come to know about you, I admire .” “Oh.” Once again, he’d rendered her speechless. What could she say to that? “Guess you got flattery down pretty well, for someone who says he’s not familiar with it .” “If you say so, Lara Brooks.” He wrapped his fingers around her hand and squeezed gently. His thumb traced a delicate path from the base of her pinky to her thumb. “How did your sister come by her scar ?” Lara swallowed, chest tight. Guilt plagued her every time she saw that scar, and now that Tabitha was gone … “I gave it to her .” “Sounds like a story to be told .” “I—” Don’t want to talk about it. Can’t talk about it. But that wasn’t true, was it? Tabitha was dead, but Ronin had been right — she lived on so long as Lara kept her memories, kept her love. “Not much of a story, really. She was teaching me how to use a knife, and I was frustrated and ready to give up. She came up behind me, I think to correct my grip, but I jerked away, insisting I could do it myself. I didn’t know I cut her until I saw the blood .” Her fingers twitched against his skin. “I was a lot more careful from then on, but she never held it against me .” “She must’ve been an amazing woman .” “ She was .” “You’ve learned a lot from her. She would be proud of you .” “There you go, with the flattery again.” She looked away so he wouldn’t see how his words affected her, and though she wanted to brush them off, they sank deep into her chest. Tabitha was gone, but…things might still be okay. Whatever Lara thought about herself, Tabitha had always shown faith in her . “ Lara ?” “ Hmm ?” His hand retraced its original path, sliding back up her calf, stopping high on her leg. He brushed his thumb over the sensitive flesh on the inside of her thigh. Holding her hand captive against his chest, he leaned forward. She turned her head to him slowly, finding his face only inches from hers . “I want you .” Her eyes widened and her mouth opened in a circle, but she couldn’t respond. Those three words, so small, so simple, made her breath ragged and her blood hot. She recalled the explosion of sensation he’d awoken in her. After the crushing news he’d delivered, she needed a distraction, but he was so much more than that . “I want you, too,” she said, and he kissed her before the last word was fully past of her lips . Placing a hand on her back, he eased her down onto the bed. The blanket rasped over her sensitive nipples as he pulled it away. They beaded in the cool air. She raised her knees to cradle his hips as he moved over her, arching her back to press her breast into his palm . She wasn’t running from her pain, this time. Lara wanted to feel what only he could make her feel. Alive . Free . She wanted him . * * * S parks pulsed between the electrodes under Ronin’s skin, lighting all circuits with a near-overwhelming hum. Had he known what touching her would be like, he’d have succumbed to his desire that first night, and run his fingertips over her delicate, responsive flesh. His skin could relay temperature and touch, but hers reacted and changed . He could feel the little bumps on her arms when she wrapped them around his neck. Could feel her skin warming as blood rushed to its surface and pinkened it. Her nipples hardened under his gentle caress, smooth flesh giving way to the soft swelling of her areola. Her lips were hungry against his, nibbling and sucking . Ronin broke the kiss and pulled away. Whether it was result of her grief or not, he would experience all of her that he could while she desired him. He wanted to explore the many ways in which he could coax reactions from her body . Leaning back on his haunches, he responded to her confused expression with a smile and slowly slid his hands over her breasts, down the flat plain of her stomach, and around her flared hips. There he stopped them, brows falling low as he examined the purple bruises on her skin. He turned his hand to cover one of the marks. The similarity was uncanny . “I hurt you,” he said. Though they’d rescinded on the no touching condition of their agreement, he never intended for her to come to harm. Especially not at his hands . “What?” she asked, breathless, and shifted her pelvis against him. It sent a ripple of sensation through him that jarred him from his thoughts for an instant . “Bruises. You’re bruised, from my hands .” Lara lifted her head, glancing down with half-lidded eyes. Her kiss-swollen lips turned up in a smile. She dropped her head back down, her torso shaking with barely-suppressed laughter. “Totally worth it .” He lifted his hands, staring at them. His processors whirred, but nothing came. Nothing but the evidence before him . “Don’t stop, Ronin. It doesn’t hurt. I didn’t even know they were there until you said something.” Somehow, her cheeks reddened further. “Besides, I kinda like ‘ em .” “I…” Don’t understand . “It’s proof you let go and felt . That you were alive, with me .” Alive . Regardless of what the truth was, no matter what doubts he harbored about himself, she saw him as alive . He settled his palms over the bruises, rubbing gently. Lara’s lips parted. She exhaled softly and closed her eyes. Ronin slid his hands down over her buttocks, then along her outer thighs, stopping at the soft undersides of her knees . Guiding her legs up and spreading them wider, he focused his optics on her glistening sex. His memory called up the feel of Lara sucking at his lip, and his sensors recreated the feel of it, though it was only a muted echo without the actual touch of her mouth . Internal systems he hadn’t activated in decades fired to life. He opened his mouth and drew in air. The soft vibration of the pump in his abdomen was strange after so long. It ran for several seconds, long enough for his diagnostics to confirm there were no leaks. The seals were still in place . He expelled the air as he dropped his head . “Ron—” She finished his name in a high- pitched gasp . Pressing his lips to her labia, he parted them with his tongue, spreading her natural moisture. The pump shifted on again, generating just enough suction to raise her budded clitoris. When he licked it, Lara moaned, bucking her hips against his face . He wrapped his arms around her thighs to keep her from squirming away . Her heels pressed into his shoulders, and as he continued to move his tongue, she put her hands on his head. She writhed in his hold, nails scraping over his scalp. His audio receptors picked up the rapid thump of her heart through her legs, punctuated by her panting cries . Every motion, every sound, was a declaration of life . “Oh God! Ronin !” Her body tensed, and liquid heat flowed from her. She curled her fingers and tugged his hair, creating prickling points of pain that crackled and faded over a fraction of a second . Finally, Lara sagged, her hands falling away. Ronin lifted his head to look at her. She stared back at him with sparkling eyes, her chest heaving, hair disheveled, and skin flushed . She raised her arms and beckoned him closer . Ronin released her thighs and pulled himself up, his abdomen brushing over her slick folds. She shivered. Bracing himself on his elbows, he settled a small portion of his weight atop her, her breasts against his chest . “Didn’t know you could do that ,” she said, wrapping her limbs around him. She rocked her pelvis forward to rub over his shaft . “Been no reason to, until now .” A smile parted her lips as she slid her hands around his shoulders, across his chest, and down his belly, trailing heat in their wake. His processors simulated a dozen possible sensations to determine what her hand would feel like on his penis. When she finally closed her fingers around it and guided it into her welcoming body, everything stopped. Thought processes halted, analysis was suspended; there was only pure sensory input, untainted by interpretation. Only the pleasure of her touch as their bodies united . Moist heat enveloped him as her inner muscles drew him deeper. Her hands roamed up, over his buttocks, following the contours of his back, and finally settled on his shoulders . He pumped his hips slowly, movements measured and even. Sparks crackled at the base of his spine, fanning through his body. Lara stared up at him, and he stared back, noting every tiny change in her — the slight drooping of her eyelids each time he slid in, juxtaposed by her lips parting further, the way her grip tightened, pressing her nails into his skin, somehow enhancing his pleasure . Lara dug her heels into the backs of his thighs and met his thrusts, taking him deeper and harder each time. Electric bursts coursed through him. Her movements became erratic as her breathing grew more ragged, broken by her soft moans . Ronin didn’t act on logic or careful calculations; mutual pleasure was his only motivator, the only factor he considered. Increasing his pace, he balanced his weight on one arm and wrapped the other around her waist, lifting her bottom off the bed. The change in angle brought new sensations. The feel of her altered, the points of friction shifted, and her cries became frantic. She dragged her nails down his back and clung to him . The urgency of her movements fled as her body stiffened, and she screamed. She quivered in his arms, her sex rippling, tightening on his penis and blasting it with heat . His processors buzzed, internal systems crackling out as Lara pushed his sensors into overload. He pressed on, eliciting another cry of pleasure from her, and then his optic input flashed white. The world fell away. Every minute movement from her inner muscles played across his shaft with the force of a windstorm. He was aware of her pulse — rapid but steady — everywhere their bodies touched. She writhed beneath him, arching her back. Ronin’s body locked. Her breath was warm on his skin . Slowly, the tension in her fingers eased. She dropped her hands, and her torso sagged down onto the bed, leaving a sheen of sweat on his chest. As Ronin’s optics came back online, he lowered her fully . Draping a forearm over her eyes, she panted, a broad smile on her face. Her flushed skin glistened with perspiration . He held himself over her, not yet withdrawing. After crossing into the White, her warmth and softness was comforting, a tether back to reality as much as it was a reminder of their shared pleasure. There was no tension evident in her, no fear. No disgust for having coupled with a bot again. For a few moments, at the very least, he’d made her happy . Something about that was…fulfilling. Ronin’s brow furrowed. Why should his contentment be derived from her enjoyment? He was a dustwalker, a wanderer, a robot who would walk the wastes until he was disassembled or deactivated. And yet, after so many years of searching, it was only here with this woman that he was closest to discovering his purpose . His thoughts were interrupted by a long, low gurgling. Lara peeked at him from behind her arm . “What was that?” he asked . “My stomach .” He leaned back and moved his hands to her abdomen, checking for more bruises, feeling for sign of damage. She squirmed, body shaking. He was causing her more pain . “What are you doing?” She took hold of his wrists, pushing them away. Her smile hadn’t faltered . “I hurt you,” he replied, tilting his head . She shook again, and this time, laughter bubbled from her. “No, you didn’t .” The both looked at her abdomen when the sound came again . “How could I not have ?” “That means I’m hungry , Ronin .” Tentatively, he pressed his palm to her stomach. She didn’t stop him. He could feel the rumbling inside. Whatever data his fractured memory possessed on human anatomy, it apparently didn’t cover their bodily functions with much depth . “You eat, and this stops ?” “ Yup .” “So…there’s no pain ?” She looked away . “ Well …” He withdrew his hand immediately . “ Lara …” She chuckled. “Pain in all the right places .” “I don—” He snapped his mouth shut as he recalled Lara digging her nails into his skin, pulling his hair, pressing her heels into his thighs. Each a small source of pain that had heightened the experience. “I think I understand. Somewhat.” His optics dropped to where their bodies were still connected. He lowered a hand to her hip, settling his fingers over the bruises. “Pain isn’t always bad…because pain is part of being alive .” “Yeah, as long as it’s not too much.” Her smile wavered, and something flashed in her eyes for an instant. Before he could guess its nature, she recovered. “I’m fine, Ronin. Just starving. Not, like, for real.” She pulled away from him and sat up. He immediately craved her heat. “Lemme get something to eat, and you can show me the scrap you found while you were gone .” “All right.” He watched Lara slip out of bed, noting how her figure had filled out subtly since she started living with him. He knew she wasn’t fine, not under the surface, and how could she be? Not all pain was physical. It had only been nine and a half hours since he told her about Tabitha . She plucked a shirt off the floor and tugged it on, glancing over her shoulder to cast another smile at him. Reaching up to tuck some of her loose hair behind her ear, she walked into the hallway, leaving his vision when she turned onto the stairs . Ronin climbed out of bed, pulled on his pants, and picked up his coat. As he walked, he dipped a hand into the inside coat pocket, withdrawing the ring . Perhaps it would be an uplifting surprise for her, on the heels of so strong a loss .
